name: Optus Bank
description_short: Certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) offering personal and business banking services with a 100+ year focus on serving underserved and minority communities.
description_long: Optus Bank was originally established in 1921 as Victory Savings Bank, making it South Carolina's first Black-owned bank. The institution has maintained its community-focused mission through multiple iterations, including a revitalization in 1999 as South Carolina Community Bank and a rebranding to Optus Bank in 2019. Today, it remains one of only 22 Black-owned banks in the U.S. and the sole Black-owned bank operating in South Carolina, headquartered in Columbia with corporate offices at 1501 Gervais Street. Optus Bank offers comprehensive banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, business banking services, Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), Certificates of Deposit (CDs), high-impact loans, and green financing options. The bank provides both in-person banking services and mobile account management capabilities. Their lending portfolio demonstrates a strong commitment to their stated mission, with 78% of loans made to minority borrowers, 51% of loans directed to small businesses, and over 96% of loans by dollar value made in persistent poverty counties. What distinguishes Optus Bank is its explicit mission-driven approach as a Certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) focused on economic empowerment and financial inclusion. The bank emphasizes personalized service combined with technology-driven innovation, free financial wellness tools, and financial literacy initiatives centered on economic inclusion and community wealth building. Their leadership includes Benita Lefft (President and COO as of 2024), with a board that includes Chairman Paul Mitchell and investor Dominik Mjartan, reflecting long-term community commitment. As a traditional bank with CDFI certification, Optus Bank is best suited for individuals and small business owners seeking banking relationships with institutions explicitly committed to serving underserved communities. However, prospective customers should note that specific product terms, interest rates, and lending requirements are not detailed on the website, and the bank's community development focus may mean traditional banking conveniences differ from larger national institutions.
services: Mobile account management and banking app
pros: Certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) status demonstrates genuine commitment to underserved communities
78% of loans made to minority borrowers, exceeding industry averages significantly
51% of loans directed to small businesses, providing accessible capital to entrepreneurs
Over 100-year history as a continuously operating community bank since 1921
Over 96% of loans by dollar value made in persistent poverty counties, showing targeted impact
Free financial wellness tools and comprehensive financial literacy programs
Only Black-owned bank in South Carolina, one of only 22 in the entire U.S.
cons: Website does not disclose specific interest rates, fees, or APRs for any products or accounts
Limited geographic presence with only corporate office address provided; unclear branch network extent
No information provided about minimum deposit requirements, account opening procedures, or eligibility criteria
Mobile banking capabilities mentioned but no details about app features or functionality
No comparison tools or product calculators available to help customers evaluate options
best_for: Minority-owned small businesses seeking lending from a bank with proven track record of minority business support
Individuals in underserved or persistent poverty communities looking for banking relationships with explicit community commitment
Customers seeking to bank with a Black-owned financial institution with century-long community ties
Mission-aligned investors and customers prioritizing economic justice and community development banking
diagnosis: Optus Bank is best for individuals and small business owners who prioritize banking with a community-development-focused, minority-owned institution with proven commitment to underserved populations. The main caveat is that essential banking information—interest rates, fees, minimum deposits, and specific loan terms—must be obtained directly from the bank by calling 803.733.8100, as the website focuses on mission and impact rather than product specifications.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
